description: Space-themed match-3 mobile game with Flutter and Flame engine — 50 levels, bonus tiles, obstacle mechanics

userStories.title: Project scaffolding & dependencies
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Flutter project with Flame engine and all dependencies configured so that I can start building the game
acceptanceCriteria: Flutter project created with `flutter create` (package name: com.cosmicmatch.app)
pubspec.yaml includes flame, flame_audio, riverpod, flutter_riverpod, hive, hive_flutter dependencies
Folder structure created: lib/game/, lib/models/, lib/screens/, lib/providers/, lib/utils/, assets/images/, assets/audio/, assets/levels/
Main entry point renders a MaterialApp with a placeholder home screen
Minimum Android SDK set to API 26 (Android 8)
`flutter analyze` passes with no errors
App builds and runs with `flutter run`
userStories.technicalNotes: Use `flutter create cosmic_match --org com.cosmicmatch`. Add to pubspec.yaml: flame ^1.x, flame_audio, flutter_riverpod, hive_flutter. Set minSdkVersion 26 in android/app/build.gradle. Create GameWidget wrapper in lib/game/cosmic_match_game.dart extending FlameGame.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 1. Files: pubspec.yaml, lib/main.dart, lib/game/cosmic_match_game.dart, lib/screens/home_screen.dart, android/app/build.gradle.kts, assets/, test/widget_test.dart.

userStories.title: Tile model & grid data structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tile type enums and a grid data model so that the game board state can be represented and manipulated
acceptanceCriteria: TileType enum with 6 base types: planetRed, planetBlue, star, nebula, moon, comet
BonusTileType enum with: pulsar, blackHole, supernova
ObstacleTileType enum with: asteroid, iceComet, darkMatter
TileData class with properties: type, bonusType (nullable), obstacleType (nullable), row, col
BoardState class with 8x8 grid (List<List<TileData?>>), methods for get/set tile, isEmpty, randomFill
Unit tests for BoardState: grid initialization, tile placement, random fill produces valid tiles
`flutter test` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Create lib/models/tile_type.dart for enums, lib/models/tile_data.dart for TileData, lib/models/board_state.dart for grid logic. Use freezed or plain Dart classes. BoardState.randomFill() should use Random to fill empty cells with base tile types only.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 2. Files: lib/models/tile_type.dart, lib/models/tile_data.dart, lib/models/board_state.dart, test/models/board_state_test.dart.

userStories.title: Game board rendering
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see a colourful 8x8 grid of space tiles so that I can visually interact with the game board
acceptanceCriteria: BoardComponent (extends PositionComponent) renders 8x8 grid of tile components
Each TileComponent renders as a coloured shape (circle/square) with distinct colour per tile type
Grid cells are evenly spaced and centred on screen in portrait orientation
Grid resizes appropriately for different screen sizes
Board initialises with random tiles via BoardState.randomFill()
Game screen shows the rendered board via GameWidget
`flutter analyze` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Create lib/game/components/board_component.dart and lib/game/components/tile_component.dart. TileComponent extends PositionComponent, renders with Canvas paint (RectangleComponent or CircleComponent). Calculate cell size from screen width / 8 with padding. Use CosmicMatchGame.onGameResize for responsive sizing.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 3. Files: lib/game/components/board_component.dart, lib/game/components/tile_component.dart, lib/game/cosmic_match_game.dart, lib/screens/game_screen.dart, lib/screens/home_screen.dart.

userStories.title: Tile selection & swap input
userStories.description: As a player, I want to tap tiles to select and swap them so that I can make matches on the board
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping a tile highlights it with a visual indicator (glow or border)
Tapping an adjacent tile (horizontal or vertical) triggers a swap
Tapping a non-adjacent tile changes the selection to the new tile
Tapping the same tile deselects it
Swap animates tiles moving to each other's positions (smooth lerp over ~200ms)
After swap, if no match is found, tiles snap back to original positions (invalid swap)
`flutter analyze` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Add TapCallbacks mixin to TileComponent. Track selectedTile in BoardComponent. Use Flame's MoveEffect or manual position lerp in update(dt) for swap animation. After swap completes, call match detection — if no matches, reverse the swap. Adjacency check: Manhattan distance == 1.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 4. Files: lib/game/components/tile_component.dart, lib/game/components/board_component.dart, lib/models/board_state.dart.

userStories.title: Match detection algorithm
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an algorithm that detects 3+ tile matches in rows and columns so that matched tiles can be cleared
acceptanceCriteria: findMatches(BoardState) returns list of Match objects (each with list of positions and match size)
Detects horizontal matches of 3, 4, and 5+ tiles
Detects vertical matches of 3, 4, and 5+ tiles
Detects L-shaped and T-shaped matches (overlapping horizontal + vertical)
Does not match obstacle tiles or empty cells
Unit tests cover: 3-match, 4-match, 5-match, L-shape, T-shape, no-match, edge cases
`flutter test` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Create lib/utils/match_detector.dart. Scan each row left-to-right and each column top-to-bottom, tracking consecutive same-type runs. Return Match objects with positions and size. For L/T detection, merge overlapping matches that share a tile. Match class in lib/models/match.dart.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 5. Files: lib/models/match.dart, lib/utils/match_detector.dart, test/utils/match_detector_test.dart.

userStories.title: Tile clearing & gravity
userStories.description: As a player, I want matched tiles to be cleared and remaining tiles to fall down so that the board refills naturally
acceptanceCriteria: Matched tiles are removed from the board (set to null in BoardState)
After clearing, tiles above empty spaces fall down (gravity, column-based)
Empty spaces at the top of columns are filled with new random tiles
Gravity applies per-column, preserving tile order
Visual: tiles animate downward to their new positions
New tiles animate falling in from above the board
Unit tests for gravity logic: single gap, multiple gaps, full column clear
`flutter test` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Add clearMatches(matches) and applyGravity() methods to BoardState. Gravity: iterate each column bottom-to-top, shift non-null tiles down, fill top with randomFill(). In BoardComponent, animate tile position changes with MoveEffect. Trigger gravity after match clear, then re-render.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 6. Files: lib/models/board_state.dart, lib/game/components/board_component.dart, test/models/gravity_test.dart.

userStories.title: Cascade chain reactions
userStories.description: As a player, I want falling tiles to trigger new matches automatically so that satisfying chain combos occur
acceptanceCriteria: After gravity fills the board, match detection runs again
If new matches are found, they are cleared and gravity re-applied (loop)
Cascade continues until no new matches exist
Each cascade step has a brief delay for visual clarity (~300ms)
Cascade combo counter tracks chain length
Board is locked from input during cascade resolution
`flutter analyze` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Implement game loop in BoardComponent: swap → detect → clear → gravity → detect → ... until stable. Use async/await with Future.delayed for cascade timing. Set a `processing` flag to ignore taps during cascade. Track cascadeCount for scoring multiplier.

userStories.title: Score system & HUD
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see my score update as I make matches so that I feel rewarded for good plays
acceptanceCriteria: Score calculated: 3-match = 50pts, 4-match = 150pts, 5-match = 500pts
Cascade multiplier: each chain step multiplies points by chain depth (x1, x2, x3...)
HUD overlay shows current score at top of game screen
Score animates/counts up when points are awarded
HUD built as Flutter overlay widget (not Flame component) for crisp text rendering
`flutter analyze` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Create lib/providers/game_state_provider.dart with Riverpod StateNotifier for score, moves, goal progress. HUD as Flutter widget in lib/screens/game_hud.dart, rendered via Flame's overlayBuilder. Score calculation in lib/utils/score_calculator.dart.

userStories.title: Level data model & loader
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a level configuration system so that each level can define unique goals, move limits, and obstacles
acceptanceCriteria: LevelConfig class with: id, galaxyIndex, goalType (clearCount, reachScore, clearObstacles), targetTileType (nullable), targetCount, moveLimit, obstacles list, gridLayout (nullable)
GoalType enum: clearCount, reachScore, clearAllObstacles
JSON-based level definitions loaded from assets/levels/
LevelLoader reads and parses level JSON files
Unit tests for LevelConfig parsing from JSON
At least 3 test level configs created for development
`flutter test` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Create lib/models/level_config.dart and lib/utils/level_loader.dart. JSON structure: {id, galaxy, goalType, target, moveLimit, obstacles: [{type, positions}], grid: [[int]]}. Use rootBundle.loadString to read from assets. Register assets/levels/ in pubspec.yaml.

userStories.title: Move counter & goal tracking
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see my remaining moves and progress toward the level goal so that I can strategise my plays
acceptanceCriteria: Move counter decrements by 1 after each valid swap (not invalid swaps)
Move counter displayed in HUD
Goal progress tracked based on level's goalType (tiles cleared of target type, score reached, obstacles cleared)
Goal progress displayed in HUD with target vs current
Moves and goal state managed in game state provider
`flutter analyze` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Extend game_state_provider.dart to track movesRemaining and goalProgress. Decrement moves in the swap handler after a valid match. Update goal progress in the match clearing logic based on LevelConfig.goalType. Update game_hud.dart to show moves and goal.

userStories.title: Win/lose detection & overlay screens
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see a victory or defeat screen when a level ends so that I know my result and can continue or retry
acceptanceCriteria: Win detected when goal is met (regardless of remaining moves)
Lose detected when moves reach 0 without meeting goal
Level complete overlay shows: star rating (1-3 based on remaining moves), final score, 'Next Level' and 'Replay' buttons
Level failed overlay shows: 'Replay' and 'Quit' buttons
Star rating: 3 stars if >50% moves remain, 2 stars if >25%, 1 star otherwise
Board input locked when level ends
`flutter analyze` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Create lib/screens/level_complete_overlay.dart and lib/screens/level_failed_overlay.dart as Flutter widgets. Register as Flame overlays. Check win/lose conditions after each move resolves (post-cascade). Star calculation in lib/utils/star_calculator.dart.

userStories.title: Home screen & navigation
userStories.description: As a player, I want a home screen with a Play button so that I have a clear entry point to the game
acceptanceCriteria: Home screen with game title 'Cosmic Match', Play button, and Settings button
Play button navigates to level select screen
Settings button opens settings overlay (sound toggle placeholder)
Space-themed background (dark with simple star pattern)
Navigation uses Flutter Navigator (not Flame overlays) for screen transitions
`flutter analyze` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Create lib/screens/home_screen.dart. Use Navigator.push for screen transitions. MaterialApp routes: / → HomeScreen, /levels → LevelSelectScreen, /game → GameScreen. Style with space theme colours (dark blues, purples, white text).

userStories.title: Level select screen
userStories.description: As a player, I want a galaxy-themed level select screen so that I can choose which level to play and see my progress
acceptanceCriteria: Grid or scrollable list of level buttons (numbered 1-50)
Levels grouped by galaxy (10 per galaxy), with galaxy name headers
Unlocked levels show star rating (0-3 stars) earned
Locked levels shown as greyed out / locked icon
Level 1 always unlocked; subsequent levels unlock on completion of previous
Tapping unlocked level navigates to game screen with that level loaded
`flutter analyze` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Create lib/screens/level_select_screen.dart. Use GridView.builder for level buttons. Read progress from Hive (or default state if no storage yet). Galaxy names: Lyra, Orion, Andromeda, Centaurus, Pegasus. Pass levelId to GameScreen via Navigator arguments.

userStories.title: Local storage with Hive
userStories.description: As a player, I want my level progress and settings saved locally so that I can resume where I left off
acceptanceCriteria: Hive initialised in main.dart before runApp
LevelProgress model: levelId, stars (0-3), highScore, completed (bool)
ProgressRepository with methods: saveProgress, getProgress, getAllProgress, getHighestUnlockedLevel
Settings stored: soundEnabled (bool), musicEnabled (bool)
Progress saved automatically on level completion
Level select screen reads from Hive to show stars and unlock state
Unit tests for ProgressRepository
`flutter test` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Create lib/models/level_progress.dart with Hive TypeAdapter. Create lib/repositories/progress_repository.dart. Init Hive in main(): await Hive.initFlutter(); Hive.registerAdapter(LevelProgressAdapter()); Open boxes: 'progress' and 'settings'. Provide via Riverpod.

userStories.title: Bonus tile creation
userStories.description: As a player, I want special bonus tiles to be created from large matches so that I have powerful strategic options
acceptanceCriteria: 4-in-a-row match creates a Pulsar tile at the match position
L-shaped or T-shaped match creates a Black Hole tile at the intersection
5-in-a-row match creates a Supernova tile at the match position
Bonus tile replaces one of the matched tiles (remains on board after clear)
Bonus tiles visually distinct: Pulsar (pulsing glow), Black Hole (dark swirl), Supernova (bright burst)
Unit tests for bonus tile creation logic per match pattern
`flutter test` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Extend match_detector.dart to classify match size/shape. Add bonus tile creation in the clear step: if match.size == 4 → pulsar, L/T → blackHole, 5+ → supernova. Set TileData.bonusType on the remaining tile. Update TileComponent rendering for bonus visual indicators.

userStories.title: Bonus tile activation effects
userStories.description: As a player, I want bonus tiles to trigger powerful clearing effects when matched so that big combos feel rewarding
acceptanceCriteria: Pulsar: when matched, clears entire row it's in
Black Hole: when matched, clears 3x3 area centred on it
Supernova: when matched, clears all tiles of one randomly chosen type on the board
Bonus activation happens before gravity (cleared tiles fall together)
Bonus-cleared tiles contribute to score and goal progress
Chain: bonus activation can trigger other bonus tiles
Unit tests for each bonus activation effect
`flutter test` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Create lib/utils/bonus_activator.dart. Called during the clear step when a matched tile has bonusType set. Pulsar: clear all tiles in tile.row. BlackHole: clear tiles in [row-1..row+1][col-1..col+1]. Supernova: pick random type, clear all tiles of that type. Handle chain activations recursively.

userStories.title: Obstacle tiles
userStories.description: As a player, I want obstacle tiles that add challenge so that later levels feel progressively harder
acceptanceCriteria: Asteroid (level 5+): placed on board per level config; cleared when a match occurs adjacent to it; not swappable
Ice Comet (level 15+): frozen tile that requires 2 adjacent matches to clear (first match cracks, second clears)
Dark Matter (level 30+): immovable blocker, cannot be cleared, cannot be swapped — must play around it
Obstacles rendered with distinct visuals (rock texture, ice overlay, dark void)
Obstacle placement defined in level JSON config
Goal type 'clearAllObstacles' works with clearable obstacles
Unit tests for each obstacle mechanic
`flutter test` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Extend TileData with obstacleType and obstacleHealth (for IceComet: starts at 2). In match clearing: check adjacent cells for obstacles, decrement health, clear at 0. DarkMatter: skip in swap and match logic. Update level_config.dart to include obstacle positions. Update TileComponent for obstacle rendering.

userStories.title: Animations & visual effects
userStories.description: As a player, I want satisfying visual feedback for matches and cascades so that the game feels polished and fun
acceptanceCriteria: Match clear: tiles pop/burst with particle effect
Cascade: sequential glow effect as chain reactions resolve
Tile swap: smooth position lerp (~200ms)
Invalid swap: tiles move halfway and snap back
New tiles: fall in from above with slight bounce
Idle: subtle floating animation on tiles
Bonus tile activation: screen-appropriate flash/effect per type
`flutter analyze` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Use Flame's built-in effects: MoveEffect, ScaleEffect, OpacityEffect, ColorEffect. Create lib/game/effects/ directory for custom particle effects. Use ParticleSystemComponent for match burst particles. Sequence effects with SequenceEffect. Keep effects performant — limit particle count.

userStories.title: Audio system
userStories.description: As a player, I want ambient music and match sound effects so that the game has an immersive space atmosphere
acceptanceCriteria: Ambient space background music loops during gameplay
Match SFX: distinct sounds for 3-match, 4-match, 5+-match
Cascade SFX: rising pitch or intensity with chain depth
UI SFX: button tap, level complete fanfare, level failed sound
Sound and music independently toggleable in settings
Game fully playable with all audio muted (no audio-dependent gameplay)
Audio assets are placeholder/royalty-free sounds
`flutter analyze` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Use flame_audio package. Create lib/utils/audio_manager.dart as singleton. Preload audio in game init. Read sound/music settings from Hive settings box. Place audio files in assets/audio/. Use FlameAudio.bgm for background music, FlameAudio.play for SFX.

userStories.title: Galaxy chapters & backgrounds
userStories.description: As a player, I want each galaxy chapter to have a unique visual theme so that progression feels varied and rewarding
acceptanceCriteria: 5 galaxy chapters: Lyra, Orion, Andromeda, Centaurus, Pegasus (10 levels each)
Each galaxy has a unique background image/colour palette
Background changes when playing a level in that galaxy
Level select screen shows galaxy sections with themed headers
Galaxy backgrounds are simple gradient or parallax star fields (not complex art)
`flutter analyze` passes
userStories.technicalNotes: Create lib/models/galaxy.dart with Galaxy enum and colour palettes. Background as ParallaxComponent or gradient paint in CosmicMatchGame. Map galaxy index from LevelConfig. Update level_select_screen.dart with galaxy-themed section headers. Store palette constants in lib/utils/theme_constants.dart.

userStories.title: Level content pack (50 levels)
userStories.description: As a player, I want 50 designed levels across 5 galaxies so that there is meaningful content to play through
acceptanceCriteria: 50 level JSON configs in assets/levels/ (level_01.json through level_50.json)
Levels 1-10 (Lyra): basic match goals, no obstacles, generous move limits
Levels 5+: Asteroid obstacles introduced
Levels 11-20 (Orion): harder goals, tighter move limits
Levels 15+: Ice Comet obstacles introduced
Levels 21-30 (Andromeda): mix of goal types including clearAllObstacles
Levels 30+: Dark Matter obstacles introduced
Levels 31-50 (Centaurus, Pegasus): full difficulty, all obstacle types
Difficulty progression feels gradual and fair
All 50 levels load and are playable
userStories.technicalNotes: Create JSON files in assets/levels/. Use consistent schema from level_config.dart. Difficulty knobs: moveLimit (start 30, decrease to 15), targetCount (start 10, increase to 50), obstacle count and type. Test by loading all 50 and verifying parse. Consider a level generator script in tool/ if manual authoring is too slow.

userStories.title: App polish & Play Store prep
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the app polished and ready for Play Store submission so that it can be published
acceptanceCriteria: App icon: space-themed launcher icon (adaptive icon for Android)
Splash screen: brief branded splash on app launch
App name set to 'Cosmic Match' in AndroidManifest.xml
Release APK builds successfully: `flutter build apk --release`
App size under 50MB
GitHub Actions CI workflow: analyze, test, build APK on push
Privacy policy page (simple static page or text file)
Play Store metadata: title, short description, full description, category (Puzzle)
Screenshots captured for store listing (phone format)
userStories.technicalNotes: Use flutter_launcher_icons package for app icon generation. Add flutter_native_splash for splash screen. Create .github/workflows/ci.yml for GitHub Actions. Create store/ directory for Play Store assets. ProGuard/R8 enabled for release build. Sign with upload key for Play Store.
